text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Margaret Yuen
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE]
DATE OF REPORT: [DATE]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Thoracic Oncology]
PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]

---

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
A: Left upper lobe mass, CT-guided core needle biopsy (4 cores submitted in formalin)

CLINICAL HISTORY:
62-year-old female, former smoker (15 pack-year history, quit 8 years ago), presenting with a 3.8 cm left upper lobe pulmonary mass with associated mediastinal and contralateral lymphadenopathy on staging PET-CT. Family history significant for lung cancer (mother). Biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis and molecular profiling.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled "left upper lobe mass, CT-guided biopsy," are four tan-white cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments ranging from 0.7 to 1.4 cm in length and 0.1 cm in diameter. All four cores are submitted entirely in one block (A1). Representative sections submitted for immunohistochemistry as indicated.

Submitted: A1 (4 cores, entirely submitted)

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Histologic sections of Specimen A demonstrate fragments of lung parenchyma with infiltration by a malignant epithelial neoplasm. The tumor is composed predominantly of glandular structures arranged in an acinar pattern (estimated 60% of tumor) with areas of lepidic growth along alveolar walls (estimated 30%). A minor component shows solid nests of tumor cells without gland formation (estimated 10%). The acinar component shows well-formed gland-like structures lined by columnar to cuboidal cells with moderate nuclear enlargement and prominent nucleoli. The lepidic component demonstrates neoplastic cells spreading along the alveolar framework without stromal invasion in those zones, though the overall lesion is classified as invasive given the predominant acinar component. The solid component shows sheets of cells with moderate nuclear pleomorphism, coarse chromatin, and occasional mitotic figures (approximately 4 per 10 high-power fields). No keratinization or intercellular bridges are identified. Intranuclear pseudoinclusions are focally present. Mucin production is minimal; periodic acid-Schiff staining highlights sparse intracytoplasmic mucin in a subset of cells.

The stroma is desmoplastic with focal inflammatory infiltrate. No lymphovascular invasion is identified within the biopsy specimen. No perineural invasion is seen. No necrosis. Background lung parenchyma demonstrates mild chronic interstitial changes and scattered alveolar macrophages with anthracotic pigment, consistent with prior smoking history.

---

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:

The following immunohistochemical stains were performed on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue (block A1) using validated antibodies and appropriate positive and negative controls:

  TTF-1 (SPT24 clone):         POSITIVE, 3+ (strong diffuse nuclear staining, >90% of tumor cells)
  Napsin A:                    POSITIVE, 2+ (moderate granular cytoplasmic staining, approximately 70% of tumor cells)
  CK7 (OV-TL 12/30 clone):    POSITIVE (diffuse)
  CK20:                        NEGATIVE
  p40:                         NEGATIVE
  p63:                         NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin:               NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A:              NEGATIVE
  CD56:                        NEGATIVE

Interpretation: The immunophenotype (TTF-1+, Napsin A+, CK7+, CK20-, p40-) is consistent with pulmonary adenocarcinoma of primary lung origin. Squamous cell carcinoma and neuroendocrine differentiation are excluded by the above profile.

PD-L1 EXPRESSION (22C3 PharmDx assay, Dako/Agilent, CLIA-validated):
  Tumor Proportion Score (TPS):   15%  (weak to moderate membranous staining in 15% of viable tumor cells)
  Combined Positive Score (CPS):  22   (includes PD-L1 staining in tumor cells, lymphocytes, and macrophages)
  Interpretation: Low positive expression (TPS 1-49%).

ALK (D5F3 clone, Ventana BenchMark ULTRA, FDA-approved companion diagnostic):
  Result: NEGATIVE
  Interpretation: No ALK protein overexpression detected. ALK rearrangement not supported by IHC.

ROS1 (D4D6 clone, Cell Signaling Technology):
  Result: NEGATIVE
  Interpretation: No ROS1 protein overexpression detected.

---

MOLECULAR / ANCILLARY STUDIES:

EGFR Mutation Analysis (PCR-based, cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2, Roche Molecular Systems -- FDA-approved):
  Result: MUTATION DETECTED
  Specific alteration: L858R point mutation, exon 21
  Interpretation: Sensitizing EGFR mutation identified. This mutation predicts sensitivity to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors. No exon 19 deletion, exon 20 insertion, or T790M detected on this assay.

Note: Comprehensive genomic profiling (NGS, 500+ gene panel) ordered separately; results to follow under separate accession.

KRAS mutation testing: Not performed (deferred to NGS panel per institutional reflex protocol).

---

DIAGNOSIS:

A.  LEFT UPPER LOBE MASS, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

    INVASIVE ADENOCARCINOMA, LUNG PRIMARY
    - Mixed histologic pattern: acinar predominant (approximately 60%), with lepidic (approximately 30%) and solid (approximately 10%) components
    - TTF-1 positive (3+), Napsin A positive (2+); immunophenotype consistent with pulmonary primary
    - CK7 positive, CK20 negative, p40 negative; squamous and neuroendocrine differentiation excluded
    - PD-L1 TPS 15% (22C3 PharmDx); CPS 22
    - ALK (D5F3 IHC): NEGATIVE
    - ROS1 (IHC): NEGATIVE
    - EGFR L858R mutation (exon 21): DETECTED by PCR-based companion diagnostic assay
    - No lymphovascular invasion identified in biopsy specimen
    - Comprehensive NGS profiling pending (separate report)

---

COMMENT:

The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings confirm a primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma with acinar-predominant architecture. The lepidic component, while present, does not qualify this as a minimally invasive or in situ lesion given the dominant invasive acinar and solid growth. The TTF-1 and Napsin A co-positivity strongly supports lung primary, and the negative squamous and neuroendocrine markers exclude other subtypes. The identification of an EGFR L858R sensitizing mutation by companion diagnostic PCR assay is a clinically actionable finding; reflexive comprehensive NGS has been ordered per our institutional lung cancer profiling protocol and results will be reported separately. PD-L1 TPS of 15% places this tumor in the low-positive category (TPS 1-49%). ALK and ROS1 IHC are both negative, consistent with typical EGFR-driven adenocarcinoma where these alterations are mutually exclusive.

Adequacy of specimen: Adequate for diagnosis and molecular testing. Tumor content estimated at approximately 55-60% of cellular material in submitted cores.

---

Electronically signed by: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]

Technician: [Histotechnologist initials]
Case reviewed with Thoracic Pathology fellow present.

--- END OF REPORT ---
